metadata.purpose: Provide a complete, repo-ready instruction set for continuing Neural Search after the v0.5 graph and retrieval infrastructure work. This file splits tasks between Claude and Codex, defines acceptance criteria, establishes quality gates, and maps the next major development phases.

completed.item: Scientific label extraction exists with approximately 190 or more rules.
Analysis affordance detection exists with approximately 16 initial affordance types.
Embedding infrastructure exists with providers and field indexes.
Knowledge graph schema, builder, query engine, search features, reports, and experimental design seed matching exist.
Graph docs exist: KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH_SCHEMA.md, GRAPH_AUGMENTED_SEARCH.md, EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN_GRAPH.md, V0_5_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.
145 tests were reported passing after Claude v0.4 and Codex v0.5 work.

currentState.mainGap: The repo has strong components, but they are not yet fully unified into a single canonical retrieval path. The next milestone is to connect normalized corpus records, field embeddings, graph artifacts, query intent parsing, hard-negative constraints, and evaluation metrics into one living scientific retrieval engine.

northStar.vision: Neural Search should become a provenance-backed scientific retrieval and experimental design engine. It should help scientists and AI research agents find datasets, papers, analysis affordances, reusable signals, linked experiments, missing metadata, and future study opportunities.
northStar.exampleQuery: Find datasets where mice perform probabilistic reversal learning with OFC or striatum recordings, reward omission events, trial outcomes, and enough metadata to fit a Q-learning model. Then show related papers, reusable datasets, missing controls, and possible follow-up experiments.

expectedCapabilities.capability: Return datasets, papers, and graph neighborhoods, not only prose summaries.
Explain why each result matches using evidence-backed labels and score breakdowns.
Link datasets to papers, tasks, modalities, brain regions, species, behavioral events, and analysis methods.
Support analysis affordance queries such as Q-learning, decoding, event alignment, population dynamics, and connectivity.
Support hard-negative constraints such as "exclude fMRI", "without EEG", and "mouse but not rat".
Produce measurable benchmark improvements with transparent evaluation metrics.

agentOperatingRules.rule: Do not rewrite the project from scratch. Extend the current architecture incrementally.
Keep public APIs backward-compatible unless a change is explicitly documented and tested.
Every new scientific label, graph edge, affordance, or dataset claim should include provenance or a clear route to provenance.
Prefer deterministic fixtures and hashing embeddings in CI. Optional local or external embedding providers must not be required for tests.
Hard-negative constraints must be implemented as filters when explicit, not merely as score penalties.
Every task must include tests, docs or doc updates where applicable, and command-level validation.
Claude owns scientific breadth, benchmark design, ontology expansion, and documentation. Codex owns backend integration, CLIs, Makefile targets, tests, and correctness.
When both agents touch the same feature, Claude should define the scientific specification first and Codex should implement against it.
Do not add heavyweight model dependencies to the default installation path unless behind an optional extra.
Always report changed files, commands run, metrics, failures, and unresolved questions at handoff.

primaryResponsibilities.item: Analysis affordance definitions and evidence rules.
Benchmark query design and human relevance rubrics.
Dataset card language and scientific explanation templates.
Experimental design templates and graph reasoning examples.

avoid.item: Search engine integration changes unless specifically asked.
Changing Codex-owned implementation files without coordination.

primaryResponsibilities.item: Canonical normalized corpus conversion and artifact building.
Graph artifact loading and graph reranking.
Intent router and hard-negative constraint parser.
Evaluation metrics, CLIs, tests, fixtures, and Makefile targets.

avoid.item: Expanding scientific ontology without Claude specification.
Writing broad scientific roadmap docs unless they are implementation docs.
Replacing existing benchmark suites without preserving historical comparability.

milestone.name: Canonical Scientific Retrieval Engine
milestone.summary: Unify normalized corpus, embeddings, graph, intent routing, hard-negative constraints, scoring, explanations, and evaluation into one coherent retrieval path.

successCriteria.criterion: One canonical normalized demo corpus exists and is used by downstream artifacts.
Persistent field embeddings can be built and loaded.
Persistent graph artifact can be built from normalized records.
Main search includes graph_score and field_semantic_score when enabled.
Hard-negative constraint violation rate is 0 percent for explicit negative constraints.
Evaluation supports dataset recall, precision, MRR, NDCG, label recall, constraint violation rate, and graph-link precision.
Benchmarks include dataset search, adversarial search, paper-to-dataset linking, analysis affordance search, graph reasoning, and experimental design queries.

milestone.name: Real Corpus and File Inspection Layer
milestone.summary: Expand from demo fixtures into real public data sources and inspect actual file structures such as NWB, BIDS, events.tsv, trials tables, units tables, electrodes tables, and behavior metadata.

successCriteria.criterion: Ingest 50 to 100 DANDI datasets.
Ingest 50 to 100 OpenNeuro datasets.
Ingest 500 to 2000 neuroscience papers from a paper metadata source.
Extract file-level usability claims and evidence-backed analysis affordances.
Generate dataset cards from inspected metadata.

milestone.name: AI Research Workflow Engine
milestone.summary: Add agent-facing APIs and workflows for dataset discovery, paper-to-dataset linking, dataset comparison, notebook generation, and experimental design.

successCriteria.criterion: Agent-facing search API exposes score breakdown, graph context, missing metadata, and linked papers.
Paper-to-dataset API identifies used, mentioned, similar, and extension datasets.
Experiment design API returns prior datasets, prior papers, analysis plan candidates, missing controls, and risk flags.
Notebook generation templates exist for NWB, BIDS, calcium imaging, ephys, behavior, and multimodal alignment.

milestone.name: Latent Neural and Behavioral Search
milestone.summary: Add file-derived neural and behavioral feature summaries, then learned latent embeddings for similarity search across experiments beyond metadata.

successCriteria.criterion: Extract deterministic neural summaries for ephys, calcium, EEG, ECoG, and fMRI where possible.
Extract deterministic behavioral summaries from trials, events, kinematics, pose, and stimulus metadata.
Build separate neural_summary_embedding and behavior_summary_embedding indexes.
Prototype learned latent embeddings from self-supervised or contrastive objectives.

phase.name: Stabilize v0.5 and Create Canonical Artifact Path
phase.goal: Make normalized corpus records the source of truth for demo datasets, demo papers, graph artifacts, field embeddings, generated reports, and benchmarked search.

task.title: Create demo seed to normalized corpus converter
task.description: Add a conversion module that converts existing demo seed YAML files into normalized JSONL records. The normalized corpus should be larger than the current tiny fixture and should preserve all labels, modalities, tasks, species, brain regions, analysis affordances, source IDs, and linked paper information.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Normalized demo dataset corpus contains all demo datasets, not only two fixture records.
Normalized paper corpus contains all demo paper records.
Converter is deterministic.
Records validate against existing or newly added normalized schema checks.
Tests cover missing fields, label preservation, linked paper preservation, and deterministic output.

task.title: Add corpus, graph, embedding, and report Makefile targets
task.description: Add high-level commands so the entire artifact pipeline is reproducible from the repo root.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: make artifacts-build builds corpus, graph, reports, and embeddings in the correct order.
Targets are CI-safe by default.
Targets do not require network access or paid embedding providers.

task.title: Build persistent graph artifact from normalized corpus
task.description: Use the existing graph builder to generate a persistent graph artifact from normalized dataset and paper records.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Graph artifact includes dataset, paper, task, modality, region, species, and analysis affordance nodes.
Graph artifact includes provenance-backed edges where available.
Reports are generated without import warnings or duplicate module warnings.
Tests confirm graph artifact can be loaded by search code.

phase.name: Intent Router and Hard-Negative Filtering
phase.goal: Parse user query intent and explicit constraints before ranking. Explicit negative constraints should filter candidates, not simply lower their scores.

task.title: Implement query intent router
task.description: Implement an intent router that classifies queries into retrieval profiles. The router can be rule-based initially, but must return a structured object with intent, confidence, positive constraints, negative constraints, and scoring profile.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Intent router handles common scientific retrieval requests.
Intent router returns deterministic output for tests.
Intent profile can change field weights and reranking behavior.

task.title: Implement hard-negative constraint parser
task.description: Implement explicit negative constraint parsing. Queries containing exclude, excluding, without, not, no, but not, or uppercase NOT should filter candidates that violate the constraint.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Explicitly excluded properties never appear in returned results unless user disables hard filters.
Hard-negative violation rate is 0 percent on adversarial constraints.
Search explanations include filtered constraint summaries.

task.title: Add intent-specific scoring profiles
task.description: Add configurable scoring weights based on query intent.
task.suggestedConfig:
intent_profiles: analysis_affordance_search: lexical: 0.10 ontology: 0.20 semantic: 0.15 graph: 0.20 analysis_fit: 0.25 usability: 0.10
negative_constraint_search: lexical: 0.10 ontology: 0.25 semantic: 0.10 graph: 0.10 negative_constraint: 0.35 usability: 0.10
paper_to_dataset_linking: lexical: 0.15 paper_semantic: 0.25 graph: 0.30 provenance: 0.20 usability: 0.10

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Scores include a score_breakdown dictionary.
Profile weights are tested and documented.

phase.name: Field Embedding Retrieval Integration
phase.goal: Use field-specific embeddings in main retrieval rather than only global blobs or optional infrastructure.

task.title: Create field semantic search index loader
task.description: Add a module that loads field embedding caches, computes query embeddings with the configured provider, scores selected fields, and falls back gracefully if no embedding cache is available.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Search can include field_semantic_score.
Search explanations list matched semantic fields.
Hashing embeddings remain the default CI-safe provider.
Missing embedding files do not break lexical or ontology search.

task.title: Add embedding ablation report
task.description: Extend existing ablation tooling to compare lexical, ontology, dense field embeddings, graph, and combined retrieval.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Ablation can run without external embedding providers.
Report compares each retrieval head and the combined score.

phase.name: Graph-Augmented Reranking
phase.goal: Make graph context a first-class scoring and explanation signal.

task.title: Integrate graph score into main search
task.description: Load the persistent graph artifact when graph search is enabled and add graph_score to the main score breakdown.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Search result score_breakdown includes graph_score when graph is enabled.
Graph scoring never crashes when graph artifact is absent.
Graph explanations identify which nodes or paths contributed.

task.title: Add graph reasoning benchmark metrics
task.description: Add evaluation support for graph path quality, linked paper precision, and graph explanation coverage.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Graph reasoning benchmark suite can evaluate graph-specific behavior.
Metrics are included in benchmark report output.

phase.name: Scientific Ontology and Affordance Expansion
phase.goal: Expand the scientific vocabulary so search handles broader neuroscience, behavior, BCI, clinical, and cognitive workflows.

task.title: Expand task ontology
task.description: Expand the ontology for major behavioral, clinical, and systems neuroscience task families.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Each task includes canonical ID, label, category, definition, synonyms, common events, and likely analysis affordances.
Ontology avoids vague labels that cannot be detected from metadata or paper methods.
New labels are covered by benchmark queries.

task.title: Expand behavior and event labels
task.description: Add event and behavior labels that are useful for determining whether a dataset supports specific analyses.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Labels are mapped to synonyms and likely source fields such as NWB trials, BIDS events.tsv, or paper methods text.
Labels are connected to analysis affordances where appropriate.

task.title: Expand analysis affordances
task.description: Add precise affordances that represent analyses a dataset can support.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Each affordance specifies required metadata fields, sufficient conditions, disqualifying missing fields, and example queries.
Affordances can be used by Codex tests and benchmark expected labels.

phase.name: Benchmark and Evaluation Overhaul
phase.goal: Make all improvements measurable and scientifically meaningful.

task.title: Create benchmark_queries_v06.yaml
task.description: Create an expanded benchmark suite with expected labels, expected datasets when known, hard negatives, and rationale.
task.querySchema: query_id: q_v06_001 query: "Find mouse reversal learning datasets with OFC recordings and reward omission events" intent: analysis_affordance_search expected_dataset_ids: - DEMO_REVERSAL_OFC_EPHYS expected_labels: tasks: - reversal_learning modalities: - extracellular_ephys brain_regions: - orbitofrontal_cortex behavioral_events: - reward_omission analysis_affordances: - q_learning_model_fitting hard_negative_dataset_ids: - DEMO_VISUAL_FMRI hard_negative_modalities: - fmri rationale: "Should retrieve datasets supporting RL model fitting with OFC neural signals and reward omission metadata."

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Queries are scientifically diverse and nontrivial.
Each query includes intent, expected labels, and hard negatives where applicable.
Dataset IDs are included when known.

task.title: Implement improved evaluation metrics
task.description: Fix recall metric behavior and add metrics that reflect hard-negative safety, graph quality, and analysis affordance quality.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Recall@k is meaningful when expected_dataset_ids exist.
Hard-negative metrics identify violations explicitly.
Benchmark reports include score breakdown summaries.

task.title: Create human relevance labeling protocol v0.6
task.description: Expand the existing human relevance labeling protocol to cover dataset retrieval, paper linking, graph paths, and experimental design recommendations.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Protocol supports 100 to 200 initial human judgments.
Labels are suitable for later learned reranker training.

phase.name: Real Corpus Expansion Strategy
phase.goal: Define the next public data ingestion plan before Codex implements it.

task.title: Create real corpus expansion strategy doc

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Doc clearly identifies source APIs, expected metadata fields, and failure modes.
Doc gives Codex enough information to implement ingestion in v0.7.

task.title: Define file inspection claim schema
task.description: Specify evidence-backed claims extracted from real files.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Claim schema supports evidence, source, confidence, extracted field, and downstream graph edge creation.

phase.name: AI Research Workflow Roadmap
phase.goal: Define how Neural Search becomes useful for next-generation AI-based research workflows.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Each workflow includes input schema, output schema, required retrieval calls, and failure modes.
Workflow design is grounded in current repo capabilities and v0.6/v0.7 roadmap.

requiredFraming.item: Neural Search is a provenance-backed research-object retrieval engine.
Neural Search links datasets, papers, analyses, metadata claims, graph paths, and experimental design opportunities.
The system should become agent-usable, not merely human-searchable.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Vision doc helps future contributors understand why graph, provenance, and analysis affordance detection matter.

phase.name: Latent Neural and Behavioral Search Preparation
phase.goal: Prepare the project for search over real neural and behavioral structure, not just metadata.

task.title: Define deterministic feature summary spec
task.description: Define summaries to extract from ephys, calcium, EEG, ECoG, fMRI, behavior, and BIDS/NWB metadata.

task.title: Prototype summary indexes after v0.7 file inspection exists
task.description: Build separate indexes for metadata, paper, graph, file structure, behavior summary, neural summary, and learned latent embeddings.

gate.name: Artifact build validation
gate.commands: make corpus-build make graph-build make graph-reports make embeddings-build make artifacts-build

gate.name: Benchmark validation
gate.commands: python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite demo_v02 python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite adversarial python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite benchmark_queries_v06

recommendedScoreBreakdown.description: Main search results should expose enough scoring structure for debugging, evaluation, and user trust.
recommendedScoreBreakdown.example: { "result_id": "DEMO_GONOGO_CALCIUM", "final_score": 0.82, "score_breakdown": { "lexical_score": 0.42, "ontology_score": 0.88, "field_semantic_score": 0.61, "graph_score": 0.19, "analysis_fit_score": 0.79, "provenance_score": 0.75, "usability_score": 0.90, "negative_constraint_score": 1.0 }, "matched_labels": { "tasks": ["go_nogo"], "modalities": ["calcium_imaging"], "species": ["mouse"], "analysis_affordances": ["event_aligned_activity"] }, "missing_metadata": ["trial_outcome_labels"], "graph_context": [ "shares go_nogo task with 4 datasets", "linked to paper on inhibitory control", "supports event_aligned_activity affordance" ], "explanation": "Matched because the dataset includes mouse Go/NoGo behavior, calcium imaging, cue and response events, and linked paper evidence." }

agentPrompts.prompt:
You are continuing Neural Search after v0.5.
Primary goal: Unify the v0.5 architecture around a canonical normalized corpus and wire graph + field embeddings into the main retrieval path.
Do not redesign the project. Preserve existing public APIs and tests.
Read first: - docs/REMAINING_IMPLEMENTATION_WORK.md if present - docs/KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH_SCHEMA.md - docs/GRAPH_AUGMENTED_SEARCH.md - docs/EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN_GRAPH.md - docs/V0_5_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md - this XML instruction set
Tasks: 1. Add a corpus conversion module that converts data/seed/demo_datasets.yaml and data/seed/demo_papers.yaml into normalized JSONL records. 2. Add Makefile targets: - corpus-build - graph-build - graph-reports - embeddings-build - artifacts-build - benchmark 3. Build: - data/corpus/normalized/demo_v05.datasets.jsonl - data/corpus/normalized/demo_v05.papers.jsonl - data/graph/neural_search_graph.demo_v05.json - data/reports/graph/ 4. Add optional graph loading to the main search path using retrieval config. 5. Add graph_score to score_breakdown without breaking existing score fields. 6. Add field embedding retrieval integration: - Load field embedding caches. - Score query against selected fields. - Use intent-specific field weights. - Fall back safely when cache is missing. - Keep hashing embeddings as the default CI provider. 7. Add hard-negative constraint parsing and filtering for: - exclude / excluding - without - not - no - but not - NOT across modalities, tasks, sources, species, behavior-only datasets, regions, recording devices, and analysis affordances. 8. Add query intent router with intents: - dataset_lookup - paper_lookup - task_search - modality_region_species_search - analysis_affordance_search - paper_to_dataset_linking - dataset_to_paper_linking - similar_dataset_search - negative_constraint_search - ambiguous_exploratory_search - experimental_design_search 9. Improve evaluation metrics: - Precision@k - Recall@k using expected_dataset_ids - MRR - NDCG@k - Label recall - Constraint violation rate - Hard-negative violation rate - Analysis affordance precision - Graph-link precision - Explanation coverage 10. Add tests for all new behavior.
Acceptance criteria: - The normalized demo corpus is no longer a 2-record fixture. - The graph artifact can be generated from normalized records. - Main search results include graph_score when graph config is enabled. - Main search results include field_semantic_score when field embeddings are enabled. - Hard-negative violations are filtered out, not merely penalized. - Existing API behavior remains backward compatible. - Tests and lint pass.
Validation commands: pytest -q ruff check neural_search tests make corpus-build make graph-build make graph-reports make embeddings-build make artifacts-build python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite demo_v02 python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite adversarial
Final handoff must include: - Summary of work completed - Commits - Files changed - Commands run - Benchmark metrics - Known failures or skipped items - Recommended next tasks

You are continuing Neural Search after v0.5.
Primary goal: Make the scientific retrieval layer broader, more rigorous, and ready for real public corpus expansion and next-generation AI research workflows.
Focus on scientific design, ontology breadth, benchmark quality, dataset/paper curation strategy, experimental design logic, and documentation. Avoid making large backend code changes unless small docs/config/test fixtures are needed.
Read first: - docs/QUERY_INTENT_AND_FIELD_ROUTING.md - docs/EMBEDDING_MODEL_STRATEGY.md - docs/HUMAN_RELEVANCE_LABELING_PROTOCOL.md - docs/KNOWLEDGE_GRAPH_SCHEMA.md - docs/GRAPH_AUGMENTED_SEARCH.md - docs/EXPERIMENTAL_DESIGN_GRAPH.md - this XML instruction set
Tasks: 1. Expand the ontology for: - reinforcement learning tasks - sensory decision-making - motor control - BCI tasks - speech/language tasks - sleep/seizure clinical tasks - social behavior - naturalistic behavior - visual/auditory processing - navigation/spatial memory - memory/attention 2. Expand behavior labels: - trial outcome - reward prediction error - omission - reaction time - kinematics - pupil/arousal - sleep stage - seizure onset - phoneme - speech onset - left/right hand imagery - stimulus identity 3. Expand analysis affordances: - q_learning_model_fitting - temporal_difference_modeling - choice_decoding - reward_prediction_error_analysis - motor_decoding - speech_decoding - seizure_detection - sleep_stage_classification - event_aligned_activity - population_dynamics - functional_connectivity - encoding_modeling - representational_similarity_analysis - pose_estimation - neural_behavior_correlation - closed_loop_decoding - session_stitching - cross_modal_alignment 4. Create data/eval/benchmark_queries_v06.yaml with at least: - 25 dataset search queries - 15 adversarial constraint queries - 15 paper-to-dataset linking queries - 15 analysis affordance queries - 10 graph reasoning queries - 10 experimental design queries 5. Create docs/REAL_CORPUS_EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md covering: - DANDI priorities - OpenNeuro priorities - paper metadata linking strategy - NWB file inspection fields - BIDS file inspection fields - dataset quality scoring - human QA protocol - known metadata failure modes 6. Create docs/AI_RESEARCH_WORKFLOW_ROADMAP.md covering: - dataset discovery agents - paper-to-dataset agents - dataset comparison agents - benchmark/audit agents - experimental design agents - notebook-generation agents - gap analysis agents 7. Create docs/HUMAN_RELEVANCE_LABELING_PROTOCOL_V06.md covering: - dataset retrieval judgments - paper linking judgments - graph path judgments - experimental design judgments - labels for exact, strong, partial, wrong task, wrong modality, wrong species, missing required data, violates negative constraint, unclear 8. Update docs/project_vision.md to frame Neural Search as a provenance-backed research-object retrieval and experimental design engine.
Acceptance criteria: - Ontology and affordance additions are evidence-aware and not vague. - Benchmark queries include expected labels, expected dataset IDs where available, and hard negatives. - Docs guide Codex implementation without ambiguity. - New ontology terms and affordance terms are suitable for test fixtures and benchmark expected labels. - Do not break existing tests.
Final handoff must include: - Summary of work completed - Files changed - New ontology/affordance counts - Benchmark query counts - Docs created or updated - Known limitations - Recommended next Codex tasks

instructionSet.handoffTemplate:
# Neural Search Agent Handoff
## Agent Claude or Codex
## Summary Brief summary of completed work.
## Files Changed - path/to/file.py: what changed - path/to/doc.md: what changed
## Commands Run ```bash pytest -q ruff check neural_search tests python -m neural_search.evaluation.run_benchmark --suite demo_v02 ```
## Results - Tests: - Lint: - Benchmarks: - Graph build: - Embedding build:
## Metrics - Precision@5: - Recall@5: - MRR: - NDCG@10: - Label recall@10: - Hard-negative violation rate: - Graph-link precision:
## Known Issues - Issue 1 - Issue 2
## Next Recommended Tasks - Task 1 - Task 2

finalNotes.note: The most important v0.6 shift is architectural unification. Do not add another disconnected subsystem until normalized corpus, graph, embeddings, search, and evaluation flow through the same canonical path.

The long-term differentiator is not generic semantic search. It is the combination of provenance-backed dataset claims, analysis affordance detection, graph reasoning, and latent neural/behavioral similarity.
The system should be designed so both humans and AI agents can ask scientifically meaningful questions and receive evidence-backed, audit-ready answers.
